title: Product Strategy (3C + P)
definition: Strategy framework: Customers, Competition, Company, and Product (4Ps: product, place, price, promotion).
whenToUse: Creating product strategy before roadmap planning.
interviewPrompts: What are the four components of product strategy?
officeUse: Strategy docs, quarterly planning, exec reviews.

title: RICE Scoring
whenToUse: Comparing multiple initiatives on a roadmap.
interviewPrompts: How would you prioritize these three features?

title: Opportunity Solution Tree Structure
definition: Outcome at root → opportunities from research → solutions → experiments.
whenToUse: Continuous discovery and aligning team on what to test next.
interviewPrompts: How does discovery connect to your roadmap?
officeUse: Weekly discovery cadence and product trio rituals.

title: Outcome vs Output
definition: Outputs are features shipped; outcomes are behavior changes achieved.
whenToUse: Setting goals, OKRs, and evaluating success.
frameworkSteps: Identify outputs that might drive it
Pivot if output doesn't move outcome
interviewPrompts: Give an example of an outcome vs output goal.
officeUse: OKR setting and success metric definition.

title: Prioritization Tradeoffs
definition: Explicitly communicating what you are NOT doing and why when prioritizing.
interviewPrompts: How do you say no to a stakeholder request?

title: Continuous Discovery
definition: Weekly customer touchpoints to iteratively test assumptions, not a one-time phase.
whenToUse: Ongoing product development after initial launch.
interviewPrompts: How do you practice continuous discovery on a busy team?

title: Roadmap Communication
definition: Presenting priorities as problems and outcomes, not feature dates.
interviewPrompts: How would you present a roadmap to executives?
